title: Trusted Delivery, Supply Chain, Progressive Release, and Rollback · Claim Defense
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for trusted delivery, supply chain, progressive release, and rollback and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for trusted progressive delivery dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bind reviewed source, protected workflow, isolated builder, inputs, dependencies, output digest, provenance, and verification.
Maintain current direct, transitive, generated, container, service, data, model, and license inventory with vulnerability ownership.
Constrain events, untrusted input, tokens, OIDC claims, secrets, runners, caches, environments, approvals, and audit evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Source Build Provenance · changed case · Quiz Source Build Provenance
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Automation rebuilds mutable inputs with broad secrets in each environment, then moves full traffic before provenance, guardrails, or rollback are verified. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Which action demonstrates Source Build Provenance without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Source Build Provenance · changed case · Quiz Source Build Provenance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind reviewed source, protected workflow, isolated builder, inputs, dependencies, output digest, provenance, and verification. For Source Build Provenance · changed case · Quiz Source Build Provenance, keep Source Build Provenance connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Rehearse compatible rollback, rollforward, configuration and data recovery, communication, and post-recovery verification.
